annotations.label: Multiplication as repeated addition

anchor.snippet: may be thought of as repeated addition

annotations.note: Repeated addition is `nsmul` and `Nat.nsmul_eq_mul : m • n = m * n` identifies it with multiplication on ℕ.

annotations.label: Commutative property (intro)

anchor.snippet: which states in this case that adding 3 copies of 4

annotations.note: `mul_comm : a * b = b * a` is the commutativity asserted (adding 3 copies of 4 equals 4 copies of 3).

annotations.label: Rectangle visualization

anchor.snippet: visualized as counting objects arranged in a rectangle

annotations.note: An informal pedagogical visualization, not a mathematical statement to formalize.

annotations.label: Product of measurements

anchor.snippet: is a new type of measurement

annotations.note: Dimensional analysis of physical measurements is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Division as inverse operation

anchor.snippet: The inverse operation of multiplication is considered division

annotations.note: `div_eq_mul_inv : a / b = a * b⁻¹` defines division as the inverse operation of multiplication.

annotations.label: Factors, multiplicand, multiplier

anchor.snippet: are generally called the factors

annotations.note: Naming terminology for the operands has no formal counterpart.

annotations.label: Product is a multiple

anchor.snippet: the product is a multiple of the other

annotations.note: `dvd_mul_right : a ∣ a * b` states a product is a multiple of each factor.

annotations.label: Product of two natural numbers

anchor.snippet: The product of two natural numbers

annotations.note: Multiplication on ℕ is the standard `*` (CommMonoid/CommSemiring), defined as iterated addition via `nsmul`.

annotations.label: Product of zero and an integer

anchor.snippet: The product of zero and another integer is always zero

annotations.note: `zero_mul : 0 * a = 0` (and `mul_zero`) hold in any `MulZeroClass`, including ℤ.

annotations.label: Rule of signs

anchor.snippet: is determined by the product of their positive amounts

annotations.note: `neg_mul`, `mul_neg`, and `neg_mul_neg : -a * -b = a * b` encode the rule of signs in any ring.

annotations.label: Product of two fractions

anchor.snippet: by multiplying their numerators and denominators

annotations.note: `Rat.mul_eq_mkRat : q * r = mkRat (q.num * r.num) (q.den * r.den)` multiplies numerators and denominators.

anchors.snippet: by multiplying their numerators and denominators

annotations.label: Product of two real numbers

anchor.snippet: is the least upper bound of the term-by-term products

annotations.note: Real multiplication exists but is built via Cauchy sequences (`Real.mul`), not the Dedekind/least-upper-bound construction described.

annotations.label: Product of two complex numbers

anchor.snippet: Two complex numbers can be multiplied by the distributive law

annotations.note: `Complex.mul_re`/`Complex.mul_im` give the distributive-law product on ℂ.

annotations.label: Imaginary unit squared

anchor.snippet: the distributive law and the fact that

annotations.note: `Complex.I_sq : Complex.I ^ 2 = -1` encodes the i² = −1 identity used in deriving the product formula.

annotations.label: Geometric meaning of complex multiplication

anchor.snippet: the magnitudes are multiplied and the arguments are added

annotations.note: `Complex.arg_mul_coe_angle` adds arguments and `norm_mul` multiplies magnitudes under complex multiplication.

annotations.label: Quaternion multiplication noncommutative

anchor.snippet: are in general different

annotations.note: Quaternions and their (noncommutative) ring multiplication are formalized, but no explicit lemma states `p*q ≠ q*p`.

annotations.label: Long multiplication

anchor.snippet: long multiplication

annotations.note: The schoolbook long-multiplication algorithm is not formalized.

annotations.label: Egyptian doubling of 13 × 21

anchor.snippet: to find the product of 13 and 21

annotations.note: Egyptian doubling/peasant multiplication is not formalized as an algorithm in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Grid method 34 × 13

anchor.snippet: An example of multiplying 34 by 13

annotations.note: The grid (box) multiplication method is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classical multiplication complexity

anchor.snippet: requires n 2 digit multiplications

annotations.note: Bit/digit complexity of multiplication algorithms is outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Harvey–van der Hoeven algorithm

anchor.snippet: an integer multiplication algorithm with a complexity of

annotations.note: The O(n log n) integer-multiplication result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplying different quantity types

anchor.snippet: but quantities of different types can be multiplied or divided

annotations.note: Multiplying physical quantities of differing dimensions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Speed times time gives distance

anchor.snippet: multiplying speed by time gives distance

annotations.note: Dimensional physics example with no formal counterpart.

annotations.label: Capital pi product notation

anchor.snippet: can be written with the product symbol

annotations.note: `Finset.prod` is the ∏ product notation over a finite index set.

annotations.label: General product notation

anchor.snippet: More generally, the notation is defined as

annotations.note: `Finset.prod s f` generalizes the indexed product over arbitrary finite index sets.

annotations.label: Empty product equals 1

anchor.snippet: the product is an empty product whose value is 1

annotations.note: `Finset.prod_empty : ∏ x ∈ ∅, f x = 1` is the empty-product convention.

annotations.label: Product of identical factors is exponentiation

anchor.snippet: a product of n factors is equivalent to exponentiation

annotations.note: `Finset.prod_const : ∏ _x ∈ s, b = b ^ #s` equates a product of identical factors with a power.

annotations.label: Associativity and commutativity of products

anchor.snippet: Associativity and commutativity of multiplication imply

annotations.note: Order-independence is baked into `Finset.prod` (CommMonoid), and `prod_mul_distrib` reflects regrouping of factors.

annotations.label: Infinite product

anchor.snippet: is defined as the limit of the product of the first n factors

annotations.note: `tprod` (∏') and `HasProd` define the infinite product as a limit of partial products.

annotations.label: Exponentiation as repeated multiplication

anchor.snippet: the resulting operation is known as exponentiation

annotations.note: `Monoid.npow` with `pow_succ : a ^ (n+1) = a ^ n * a` defines exponentiation as repeated multiplication.

annotations.label: Commutativity

anchor.snippet: The order in which two numbers are multiplied does not matter

annotations.note: `mul_comm : a * b = b * a` in a `CommMonoid`/`CommMagma`.

annotations.label: Associativity

anchor.snippet: are invariant with respect to the order of operations

annotations.note: `mul_assoc : a * b * c = a * (b * c)` in a `Semigroup`.

annotations.label: Distributivity

anchor.snippet: Holds with respect to multiplication over addition

annotations.note: `left_distrib`/`right_distrib` (a.k.a. `mul_add`/`add_mul`) give distributivity over addition.

annotations.label: Multiplicative identity

anchor.snippet: The multiplicative identity is 1

annotations.note: `one_mul : 1 * a = a` and `mul_one` in a `Monoid` make 1 the multiplicative identity.

annotations.label: Zero property

anchor.snippet: Any number multiplied by 0 is 0

annotations.note: `mul_zero : a * 0 = 0` and `zero_mul` in a `MulZeroClass`.

annotations.label: Multiplication by −1 gives additive inverse

anchor.snippet: is equal to the additive inverse of that number

annotations.note: `neg_one_mul : -1 * a = -a` (and `mul_neg_one`).

annotations.label: −1 times −1 is 1

anchor.snippet: −1 times −1 is 1

annotations.note: `neg_mul_neg : -a * -b = a * b` gives (−1)(−1)=1 with a=b=1.

annotations.label: Existence of multiplicative inverse

anchor.snippet: has a multiplicative inverse

annotations.note: `inv_mul_cancel : a⁻¹ * a = 1` in a `Group`; for fields nonzero elements have inverses (`mul_inv_cancel₀`).

annotations.label: Order preserved by positive factor

anchor.snippet: Multiplication by a positive number preserves the order

annotations.note: `mul_lt_mul_of_pos_left : b < c → 0 < a → a*b < a*c` (and the ≤ version) preserve order under a positive factor.

annotations.label: Order reversed by negative factor

anchor.snippet: Multiplication by a negative number reverses the order

annotations.note: `mul_lt_mul_left_of_neg` and `mul_le_mul_of_nonpos_left` reverse the order under a negative factor.

annotations.label: Complex numbers have no compatible ordering

anchor.snippet: do not have an ordering that is compatible with both addition and multiplication

annotations.note: No theorem states ℂ admits no compatible order; the obstruction (squares are nonneg in a `LinearOrderedRing`) exists but ℂ-specific non-orderability is not declared.

annotations.label: Noncommutativity for matrices and quaternions

anchor.snippet: is not, in general, commutative for matrices and quaternions

annotations.note: Matrix (and quaternion) multiplication is formalized as a noncommutative `Mul`/`Ring`, but no lemma explicitly asserts noncommutativity.

annotations.label: Hurwitz's theorem

anchor.snippet: Hurwitz's theorem shows that for the hypercomplex numbers of dimension 8 or greater

annotations.note: Hurwitz's classification of normed division algebras (only dim 1,2,4,8) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Peano axioms for multiplication

anchor.snippet: Peano arithmetic has two axioms for multiplication

annotations.note: ℕ multiplication satisfies the Peano recursion (a·0=0 via `mul_zero`, a·S(b)=a·b+a) underlying Mathlib's `CommMonoid ℕ` instance.

annotations.label: 1 is multiplicative identity

anchor.snippet: is a multiplicative identity because

annotations.note: `one_mul`/`mul_one` establish 1 as the multiplicative identity.

annotations.label: Multiplication axiom for integers

anchor.snippet: The multiplication axiom for integers defined this way is

annotations.note: Integer multiplication is formalized via the `CommRing ℤ` instance.

annotations.label: Deducing −1 × −1 = 1

anchor.snippet: The rule that −1 × −1 = 1 can then be deduced from

annotations.note: `neg_mul_neg` (proved from ring/distributivity axioms) yields (−1)(−1)=1.

annotations.label: Set-theoretic product of non-negative integers

anchor.snippet: can be defined with set theory using cardinal numbers

annotations.note: `Cardinal.mul_def : #α * #β = #(α × β)` defines multiplication of cardinals via the Cartesian product.

annotations.label: Group axioms under multiplication

anchor.snippet: These axioms are closure, associativity, and the inclusion of an identity element and inverses

annotations.note: The `Group` class bundles associativity, identity, and inverses (closure is total by the `Mul` operation).

annotations.label: Non-zero rationals as a group

anchor.snippet: A simple example is the set of non-zero rational numbers

annotations.note: `Field ℚ` makes the nonzero rationals (its units ℚˣ, a `Group` via `Units.instGroup`) a multiplicative group.

annotations.label: Invertible matrices form non-abelian group

anchor.snippet: the set of invertible square matrices of a given dimension

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup` (GL n R) is the group of invertible matrices under multiplication.

annotations.label: Integers under multiplication not a group

anchor.snippet: the integers under multiplication do not form a group

annotations.note: ℤ is a `CommMonoid` (not a `Group`) under multiplication, but no theorem explicitly proves it fails the group axioms.

annotations.label: Product of whole numbers as repeated sum

anchor.section: Multiplication of different kinds of numbers
anchor.snippet: is the sum of N copies of M when N and M are positive whole numbers

annotations.note: `Nat.nsmul_eq_mul : m • n = m * n` realizes the product as N repeated additions.

annotations.label: Generalization to fractions

anchor.section: Multiplication of different kinds of numbers
anchor.snippet: is by multiplying the numerators and denominators, respectively

annotations.note: `Rat.mul_eq_mkRat` defines fraction multiplication via numerator and denominator products.

annotations.label: Real numbers via rational sequences

anchor.section: Multiplication of different kinds of numbers
anchor.snippet: can be defined in terms of sequences of rational numbers

annotations.note: `Real` is constructed as `ofCauchy` (Cauchy sequences of rationals), with multiplication induced term-wise.

annotations.label: Complex numbers as ordered pairs

anchor.section: Multiplication of different kinds of numbers
anchor.snippet: as ordered pairs of real numbers

annotations.note: `structure Complex` is a pair of reals (re, im) with multiplication given by `mul_re`/`mul_im`.

annotations.label: Multiplication as ring operation

anchor.section: Multiplication of different kinds of numbers
anchor.snippet: binary operation in a ring

annotations.note: The `Ring` class equips a type with multiplication as one of its two binary operations.

annotations.label: Division as multiplication by inverse

anchor.section: Multiplication of different kinds of numbers
anchor.snippet: is the same as multiplication by an inverse

annotations.note: `div_eq_mul_inv : a / b = a * b⁻¹` defines division as multiplication by the inverse.
